What should you do if you see a suspicious SMS with a link?
idealAnswer: Read the contents carefully, do not click on the link, delete the SMS, and report it if needed.

prompt: What should you do if a suspicious pop-up is still open on your screen?
idealAnswer: Do not click or close it. Take a screenshot, disconnect from the internet if needed, and report it to IT or security staff.

prompt: What should you do if your computer mouse is moving on its own, or programs are opening by themselves without you touching anything?
idealAnswer: Stop using the computer immediately, do not type passwords, disconnect from the internet, and report it to a family member or IT/security staff.

prompt: You accidentally open a website that looks like a bank login page, but the website address looks strange. What should you do first?
idealAnswer: Do not enter usernames, passwords, OTPs, or personal details. Do not click anything else. Take a screenshot with the address, content, date, and time, keep the page open if safe, disconnect if needed, and report it.

prompt: You receive an email claiming that your account has been locked and asks you to download an attachment to verify your identity. What should you do?
idealAnswer: Do not download or open the attachment. Verify the sender, take a screenshot, keep the email as evidence, report it, and contact the real service provider through official channels.

prompt: Which evidence is MOST important to save from a suspicious WhatsApp message?
options: A. Profile picture of the sender
C. Contact name saved in your phone

prompt: When buying an item online, which information is MOST useful if you need to make a report later?
options: B. Number of likes on the advertisement

prompt: You receive a suspicious phone call from someone claiming to be a bank officer. Which evidence should you save first?
options: B. Battery percentage of your phone
C. Color of the caller's logo
D. Time you woke up that day

prompt: Why should evidence be collected before deleting a suspicious message?
options: A. To make your phone faster
B. To share it on social media
C. To help in investigation if needed later
correctAnswer: C. To help in investigation if needed later

prompt: Which of the following is the BEST example of digital evidence?
options: C. Coffee mug beside the phone

prompt: If a message is deleted from a phone app, it can never be used as evidence again.
A photo says it was taken at 10:00 PM, so it must have been taken at 10:00 PM.
If a phone is encrypted, investigators can never get any evidence from it.
If someone deletes a file and then saves many new files, the deleted file may be harder to recover.
GPS records, call logs, and chat records all match each other, but the photo time is different. The photo time is probably the most trustworthy clue.

Auntie Lim lost RM2,000 in an online scam.

Report A
I was scammed and lost RM2,000. I felt very shocked and disappointed because I worked hard to save this money. The incident caused me a lot of stress and I could not sleep well. I hope the authorities can help me recover my money and catch the scammer as soon as possible.

Report B
On 20 June 2026 at 10:30 AM, I received a WhatsApp message from 012-3456789 claiming to be from my bank. I clicked the link and transferred RM2,000. Screenshots and the transaction receipt are attached.

Which report is more complete?
options: C. Both reports are equally complete

prompt: Which information should come first in a cybercrime report?
options: A. Personal opinions; Evidence; What happened
B. What happened; Date & Time; Contact details; Supporting evidence
C. Amount lost; Favourite website; Occupation
correctAnswer: B. What happened; Date & Time; Contact details; Supporting evidence

prompt: Before submitting a cybercrime report to the Police, MCMC, CCID, or Cyber999, what should be checked FIRST?
options: A. Whether the report is at least one page long
B. Whether the report uses formal English
C. Whether the report contains sufficient details and supporting evidence
D. Whether the report is typed in a specific font
correctAnswer: C. Whether the report contains sufficient details and supporting evidence

prompt:
Two victims submitted reports after losing RM3,000 in an online scam.

Report A
Date & Time
Scammer's phone number
Description of the incident
Amount lost

Report B
Date & Time
Description of the incident
Screenshot of the scam message
Transfer receipt

Which report would be more helpful for investigators?
options: C. Both reports are equally helpful

prompt:
A victim wrote the following report:

On 20 June 2026 at 10:30 AM, I received a scam message and lost RM2,000.

Which missing detail would MOST strengthen this report?
options: B. Screenshots of the conversation and transaction receipt
C. A detailed explanation of how the victim felt after the incident
correctAnswer: B. Screenshots of the conversation and transaction receipt
